response.justification: While Bogdan is clearly a talented polyglot engineer with strong systems and performance optimization background, this role requires deep specialized expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU/accelerator architecture, and deep-learning workload optimization—none of which are evident in his experience. His background is entirely in application-level and backend software, with no evidence of hardware architecture design, chip-level modeling, or AI accelerator optimization work. This is a fundamental domain mismatch rather than a skills gap that could be bridged.
strengths: Strong performance optimization and low-latency systems experience (Dunnhumby's millions of requests/second, microservices architecture)
Demonstrated ability to work across technical stacks and master specialized domains (polyglot engineer with C/C++, D, multiple frameworks)
Current exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and emerging AI workflows at re:cinq
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, chip design, or hardware architecture experience—a core required skill for the role
No GPU architecture, AI accelerator, or SIMD/MIMD architecture background despite these being central to the position
Career trajectory entirely in application/backend software with no transition toward systems or hardware engineering domains

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP and GPU architecture expertise
No deep-learning workload or AI accelerator optimization experience
Strong low-latency systems and performance optimization background
